draft.introduction: 1 Corinthians 15 is Paul’s extended argument for bodily resurrection, containing the earliest creedal formula in Christianity (vv. 3–5) and the theology of the resurrection body. It addresses Corinthian skeptics who denied the resurrection of the dead.

translation.text: For I delivered to you as of first importance what I also received: that Christ died for our sins in accordance with the Scriptures, that he was buried, that he was raised on the third day in accordance with the Scriptures, and that he appeared to Cephas, then to the twelve.
translation.original_text: παρέδωκα γὰρ ὑμῖν ἐν πρώτοις ὃ καὶ παρέλαβον

translator_notes.note: This is the earliest preserved Christian creed, likely dating to within five years of the crucifixion. Paul explicitly states he “received” it as tradition, making it pre-Pauline and among the oldest textual evidence for Christian belief in the resurrection.
